data.title: Humble 493 hang tough with the Magnificent 7
data.description:
By Sebastian Pellejero

Nvidia and its Big Tech brethren grew their bottom lines a collective 18% last quarter, but other S&P 500 members like Delta and Corning also overcame economic challenges for an impressive 12% bump. Such rich valuations, however, will depend heavily on sustained efficiency.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Sebastian Pellejero on LinkedIn.

CONTEXT NEWS

The S&P 500 Index is down about 3% in the month through November 20 and up 11% since the start of 2025.

data.title: Ford reaffirms annual EBIT guidance after latest aluminum supplier fire
data.description:
By Nora Eckert

Ford Motor on Friday stood by its annual EBIT guidance of $6 billion to $6.5 billion for this year following another fire this week at aluminum supplier Novelis' New York plant.

Ford's stock rose about 4% during Friday's session, extending gains after the automaker reaffirmed the outlook it had already revised because of production disruptions from a devastating blaze at the supplier's plant in September.

In October, the carmaker cut its profit forecast, citing a gross hit of up to $2 billion from the incident. Ford expects to offset about $1 billion of that next year by increasing production at certain truck plants, it said in October.

Ford is a major customer of Novelis because its trucks use a largely aluminum body. The automaker indefinitely paused production of its F-150 Lightning electric pickup in Michigan after the September fire at Novelis' Oswego, New York, factory. Output of its lucrative gas-powered F-150 lineup has also been disrupted.

In October, Novelis said it planned to restart operations at the affected part of the plant by the end of December, an acceleration from its previous projection of resuming by the first quarter of 2026. The Ford statement on Friday did not outline any changes to that timeline.

"Novelis and Ford will continue to provide updates as further details become available," the statement said.

data.title: Week's Best: UBS Loses Another Big Team — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Barron's Advisor Staff

UBS continues to suffer from a stream of departures by seasoned financial advisors. Last Friday, a team that oversaw about $6 billion in assets at the Swiss bank said they had left to launch their own registered investment advisory firm. The team, led by Denis Cleary and Greg Devine, is based in Boston and Los Angeles and calls itself 71 West Capital Partners.

Among other most-read wealth management articles this week:

Three years in prison for ex-broker. A former broker and investment advisor has been sentenced to more than three years in prison for a long-running fraud that prosecutors say bilked clients out of more than $3 million. Robert Starnes, 66, of Wauwatosa, Wis., convinced more than a dozen family members, friends, and others to let him manage their retirement savings, promising to increase their nest egg through his investing strategy, prosecutors say.

Setback for advisor access to 401(k)s. Last year Fidelity issued a public statement saying it planned to block credential-sharing applications that enable external investment advisors to gain access to their clients' retirement accounts. Now Schwab is joining Fidelity in notifying clients that it prohibits the use of credential-sharing tools. Advisors like being able to manage their clients' retirement-plan assets held at their employers' administrators, but now at least two big plan administrators say those tools pose a security risk.

AI bubble fears examined. As artificial intelligence stocks start to wobble, financial pros are looking for signs of a bubble getting ready to burst. But it isn't a given. There is real underlying demand for artificial intelligence, which is already making business, medicine, law, and other fields more productive. Four advisors shared their current thinking about the future of AI investing in our latest Barron's Advisor Big Q.

Battling the scourge of gambling addiction. An adult child with a gambling addiction can wreak havoc on a family's emotional and financial well-being, and more people now suffer from gambling problems, thanks in part to the growth of sports betting apps. Financial advisors can offer support as well as asset protection and preservation strategies to help prevent more damage. Advisors say they may need to tell parents to stop offering financial support to their addicted offspring, advice they sometimes aren't ready to hear.

The art of reporting year-end performance . Financial advisors can do their clients a favor at the end of the year by having an in-depth conversation about long-term performance — not just preparing a report that adheres with regulators' guidelines. The standard SEC formula is to calculate year-to-date, one-year, three-year, five-year, and maybe since-inception returns and compare them with a standard index benchmark. But our columnist suggests ways advisors can have a much richer discussion with their clients.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Ford Says Part of Aluminum Plant Is Running Again After Second Fire
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Ford Motor said part of its aluminum supplier's plant where there have been multiple fires is back up and running.

The Oswego, N.Y., plant, which is run by Novelis and supplied aluminum to Ford, was partially leveled during a fire in September. A second fire that broke out at the same plant on Thursday has been contained, and as of Friday, the cold mill and heat treatment operations at the facility are operating, the automaker said.

The news comes a day after The Wall Street Journal reported a second large fire broke out at the plant. Thursday's fire appeared to be in the same area as the first one, The Journal reported.

Ford said it is continuing to receive material from the plant. Novelis is leveraging alternative sources, including other plants and industry peers, to mitigate the effect, Ford said.

Novelis is building a new aluminum plant in Bay Minette, Ala., which will start commissioning in the second half of next year, Ford said.

Ford also reaffirmed its full-year 2025 guidance for adjusted earnings before interest and taxes and for adjusted free cash flow.

Following the initial fire, Ford in October said it was increasing its production of certain trucks that use less aluminum. The Dearborn, Mich., company is Novelis's biggest customer at the Oswego plant, and Ford's F-150 pickup truck is one of the auto industry's biggest users of aluminum.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: FDA Approves Merck's Keytruda with Padcev to Treat Bladder Cancer — Update
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

The Food and Drug Administration approved Merck's Keytruda in combination with Padcev to treat certain adults with muscle invasive bladder cancer (MIBC).

The FDA said Friday it has approved the drug combination as a treatment for before and after bladder-removal surgery in adult MIBC patients who are ineligible for cisplatin, a chemotherapy medication.

Padcev is co-developed and commercialized by Pfizer and Astellas Pharma. Pfizer said the FDA's approval of the drug combination was granted months earlier than expected.

A recent trial demonstrated statistically significant improvements in event-free survival and overall survival for patients with previously untreated MIBC who were treated with the drug combination before and after bladder-removal surgery and pelvic lymph node dissection, compared with surgery alone, the FDA said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

Lowe's and Home Depot Customers Willing to Spend, Just Not Too Much — Market Talk
data.description: Lowe's and Home Depot each recorded higher ticket sizes in 3Q, a sign that their customers are trading up for more expensive products even as they defer larger remodeling projects due to economic uncertainty and high interest rates. Lowe's and Home Depot depend largely on homeowners, a group that skews more middle and upper income that is still willing to spend, says Piper Sandler analyst Peter Keith tells WSJ. "They still want the newer innovation stuff," says Raymond James analyst Bobby Griffin. "But they're not willing to invest the multiple thousands or six figures in some of these remodel projects," Griffin tells WSJ.(nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: Fitch Affirms Capital Manor Inc. (OR) at 'BBB-'; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-New York-21 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed the rating for the following bonds issued by the Hospital Facilities Authority of the city of Salem, Oregon on behalf of Capital Manor Inc. at 'BBB-' :

--$56.4 million revenue and refunding bonds, series 2022;

--$35.4 million revenue bonds, series 2018.

Fitch has also affirmed Capital Manor's Issuer Default Rating (IDR) at 'BBB-'.

The Rating Outlook is Stable.

The 'BBB-' rating primarily reflects Capital Manor's strong revenue defensibility, bolstered by its limited direct competition and favorably strong independent living unit (ILU) occupancy, balanced by a highly leveraged financial profile and modest cash-to-adjusted debt position.

The Stable Outlook reflects Fitch's expectation that Capital Manor will continue to generate sound operating metrics, driven by ILU occupancy stability and consistent service fee increases, which should allow the financial profile to remain resilient through the forward-looking scenario analysis, even in a stress case.

SECURITY

Pledge of, and security interest in, gross revenues of the obligated group (OG), a mortgage lien on the community's property, and a debt service reserve fund.

KEY RATING DRIVERS Revenue Defensibility - 'a'

Strong Demand with Limited Competition

Capital Manor is located in a favorable market (Salem, OR), which has good demographic indicators and limited competition. Capital Manor is the only life plan community (LPC) within 30 miles of its campus and has limited competition for services, which is a primary credit strength. The local community supports strong independent living (IL) demand despite limited draw outside of the primary service area (PSA).

IL occupancy is exceptionally strong with a five-year ILU occupancy average of 98%. Assisted Living (AL) and memory care (MC) occupancy have been stable, averaging 81% each over the past five fiscal years. Demand is sustained by a healthy real estate market with growing median home values. Capital Manor has an average entrance fee of $214,000 and an average net worth of approximately $2.3 million, supporting monthly fee increases in ILUs and ALUs of 4.95% each in 2026. The community also benefits from a sizable waitlist of approximately 400 individuals.

Operating Risk - 'bbb'

Sound and Stable Operating Profile

Capital Manor's 'midrange' operating risk reflects a history of sound and stable operations, which have been offset by higher historical capital investment. Capital Manor's net operating margin (NOM) and NOMA averaged favorable 11.4% and 30.1%, respectively, over the past five fiscal years. In fiscal 2025 (June 30; audited), Capital Manor generated a good 27.8% NOMA, which helped support adequate debt service coverage of approximately 1.9x.

Despite better NOM and NOMA ratios, Capital Manor's operating ratio has been historically weaker, which averaged 100.8% over the past five fiscal years. However, this was improved in fiscal 2025 to 93%, which Fitch views positively.

Fitch's 'midrange' assessment is further bolstered by Capital Manor's higher historical capital investment in plant, which has been robust averaging approximately 140% of depreciation expense over the past five fiscal years. Going forward, management expects capex to be approximately $3 to $3.6 million annually; focusing on routine maintenance needs, which Fitch views as manageable.

Financial Profile - 'bbb'

Very High Leverage Position; Stable Financial Profile with Manageable Capex

Capital Manor's financial profile aligns with a 'bbb' assessment, reflecting stable operations and manageable capital spending despite very high leverage. FY25 unrestricted cash and investments were about $38 million, translating to roughly 40% cash to adjusted debt which is viewed as relatively weaker for an investment grade rating. In the base case, cash to adjusted debt and MADS coverage are supported by anticipated continued improvement in operations into 2030.

Stress assumptions drive an early dip in liquidity, with cash to adjusted debt in the high 30% range, before recovery to around 55% by year five; stress case MADS coverage recovers to roughly 2x and DCOH in the mid-500s in the stress case, consistent with 'midrange' resilience. Capex slightly below depreciation after recent elevated spending supports incremental improvement in liquidity and coverage over the forward look.

Asymmetric Additional Risk Considerations

There are no asymmetric risks associated with the rating.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

--Although unanticipated, a material deterioration in cash-to-adjusted debt such that levels are sustained at or below 30%;

--Decline in operational performance leading to deterioration of operating ratio consistently above 100% and or MADS coverage near or below 1x;

--Significant softening of demand to ILU occupancy levels near 85%.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

--Substantial increase in cash flow that improves cash-to-adjusted debt closer to 75%.

PROFILE

Capital Manor is an LPC that has operated in Salem since 1963. The community currently has 295 ILUs (including apartments and duplex/triplex villa and townhome ILUs) and a licensed residential care facility (RCF) configured with 83 AL beds and 34 MC beds. Capital Manor does not have a SNF but is able to provide high levels of care in its RCF, which is open only to its residents.

Capital Manor, Inc., which owns the community, is the sole member of the OG and had total operating revenues of approximately $30.9 million in fiscal 2025. The community has been managed by Life Care Services LLC (LCS), a national senior living operator, under a management agreement since 2010; LCS employs the community's executive director. The agreement was last renewed for a term of five years in 2020 which management expects to extend for another 5 and a half years by this January.

Sources of Information

In addition to the sources of information identified in Fitch's applicable criteria specified below, this action was informed by information from DIVER by Solve.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

While certain of the NRSRO’s credit rating subsidiaries are listed on Item 3 of Form NRSRO and as such are authorized to issue credit ratings on behalf of the NRSRO (see https://www.fitchratings.com/site/regulatory), other credit rating subsidiaries are not listed on Form NRSRO (the “non-NRSROs”) and therefore credit ratings issued by those subsidiaries are not issued on behalf of the NRSRO. However, non-NRSRO personnel may participate in determining credit ratings issued by or on behalf of the NRSRO.

data.title: VP Desrochers Files To Sell 3,000 Of Horace Mann Educators Corp [HMN]
data.description:
Desrochers Mark R, Vice President of Horace Mann Educators Corp [HMN], filed a Form 144 on November 21, 2025 with the Securities and Exchange Commission proposing to sell restricted securities:

Approximate Date Of Sale11/21/25
Shares3,000
BrokerFidelity Brokerage Services

Prior to the sale of restricted stock, owners must file a Form 144 with the SEC. Upon submission of the filing to the SEC the owner is permitted to sell the shares, or any fraction thereof, within 90 days.

For a free trial, or to learn more about our insider trading analysis and data services, please visit http://www.washingtonservice.com or contact us at (301) 913-5100 or send an email to info@washingtonservice.com.

Founded in 1970, The Washington Service provides securities professionals with financial, economic and public policy research and data.
